methodsAttempted.query: site:tulane.edu Tulane University generative AI policy students ChatGPT artificial intelligence academic integrity privacy teaching guidelines
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official Tulane AI, IT, and Library pages about generative AI, academic integrity, privacy, and teaching.
Fetched the official Artificial Intelligence at Tulane University page as the central university AI guidance source.
Fetched the official Tulane IT Guide for Generative AI Platforms for privacy, security, and high-risk-use guidance.
Fetched official Tulane Library AI guide pages for academic-integrity and teaching guidance; one more course-focused LibGuide result was rejected as less central.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a central Tulane AI page, a Tulane IT generative-AI platform guide, and Tulane Library AI guide pages. Claims use the central AI page, Tulane IT guide, and Library academic-integrity guide; a course-focused plagiarism/AI LibGuide FAQ was recorded as limited and not used for broad university-wide claims.

targets.sourceTitle: IT Guide for Generative AI Platforms
AI and Academic Research: A Guide: Academic Integrity

stopConditions: Stop after the central Tulane AI page, Tulane IT GenAI guide, and Tulane Library academic-integrity AI page are fetched.
Do not infer binding course-level rules beyond the syllabus/instructor boundary stated by Tulane sources.

artifacts.sourceTitle: IT Guide for Generative AI Platforms
AI and Academic Research: A Guide: Academic Integrity

artifacts.claimText: Tulane maintains an official Artificial Intelligence at Tulane University page with Guidelines for the Ethical and Responsible Use of AI and links related AI initiatives, resources, workshops, and training.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Tulane guidance says Level 2 Internal, Level 3 Confidential, and Level 4 Restricted data should not be entered into publicly available generative AI tools, and the IT guide separately warns against entering Medium or High Risk Tulane information into public tools not covered by university licensing.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Tulane says procured generative AI tools or systems using generative AI require a security and risk review by the Information Security Office, and the IT guide says high-risk activities such as hiring, student assessments, or legal matters should not use generative AI without first consulting Tulane IT and Information Security.

citation.citationTitle: IT Guide for Generative AI Platforms
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Tulane teaching guidance says each instructor can set clear course or project AI guidelines and encourages students to describe how AI tools were used when AI is permitted; the Library academic-integrity guide tells students to review the syllabus or ask the professor about AI-tool tolerance, limitations, and citation.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Tulane University recognizes the potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to reshape the educational and research landscape. As AI continues to influence all aspects of our modern world, it is paramount that we reap its benefits while preserving the intellectual integrity and human-centered model of our university.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: All members of Tulane University have a responsibility to protect university data from unauthorized access or disclosure. Consistent with Tulane's data governance, data management, and data classification policies, data classified as Level 2- Internal, Level 3-Confidential Data, or Level 4- Restricted should not be entered into publicly available generative AI tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Guidelines for the Ethical and Responsible Use of AI, Protect Confidential Data
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: IT Guide for Generative AI Platforms
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Avoid entering Medium or High Risk Tulane information into publicly available generative AI tools that are not covered by a university licensing agreement. This includes non-public research data, unpublished papers, confidential information from research partners, financial and human resources information, student records, medical data, and any information subject to legal or regulatory safeguarding.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Protect Tulane Data and Confidential Information
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: IT Guide for Generative AI Platforms
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Appropriate privacy and security considerations must be applied to all technology solutions used by Tulane University. Any procured generative AI tools or systems utilizing generative AI tools require a security and risk review by the Tulane Information Security Office.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Guidelines for the Ethical and Responsible Use of AI, Review of AI Tools
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: IT Guide for Generative AI Platforms
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not use generative AI tools for high-risk activities (e.g., hiring, student assessments, or legal matters) without first consulting with the Tulane IT and Information Security.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Consult with IT When Uncertain of Correct Use of Gen AI

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Each instructor has the option of putting in place guidelines that make the most sense for their specific course or project, but instructions must be clear and precise. Should the use of AI be permitted, students should be encouraged to describe how AI tools were used.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Exploring AI's Educational Potential, Foster Transparency
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI and Academic Research: A Guide: Academic Integrity
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Your professor may have included in the course syllabus a statement that addresses the use of AI tools. If not, ask them. What is their level of tolerance with AI tools? Are they permitted at all? If so, to what extent? What qualifications do they place on the limitations of these tools? How should you cite your use of generative AI content?
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Using AI in Coursework, Review Your Syllabus

artifacts.notes: Narrow source-status claim supported by the official Tulane AI page.
Claim is limited to the data categories and public/unlicensed generative AI boundaries stated by official Tulane sources.
Claim combines Tulane's review requirement for procured GenAI systems with IT consultation guidance for high-risk use.
Claim avoids a default university-wide permission/prohibition rule and stays with instructor-specific guidance and student disclosure when AI is permitted.

artifacts.title: Tulane University AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Tulane has official public AI guidance through ai.tulane.edu, Tulane IT, and Tulane Libraries. The staged claims cover source status, confidential-data handling, security/risk review for GenAI systems, and instructor-specific coursework transparency.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged run did not access Canvas, Box login-gated files, or other authenticated Tulane resources.
The course-use claim is limited to instructor-specific guidance and syllabus review; this run does not assert a single university-wide default permission or prohibition rule for all coursework.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
